title: Karthigai Deepam
intro: Karthigai Deepam is one of the most sacred Hindu festivals celebrated mainly in Tamil Nadu.
description:
Karthigai Deepam is one of the most sacred Hindu festivals celebrated mainly in Tamil Nadu. The festival is dedicated to Lord Shiva and Lord Murugan (Kartikeya) and is observed on the full moon day of the Tamil month of Karthigai, usually falling in November or December. Devotees celebrate this festival by lighting countless lamps, symbolizing the victory of light over darkness, knowledge over ignorance, and positivity over negativity.

According to Hindu traditions, Karthigai Deepam is associated with the divine Jyoti (infinite light) form of Lord Shiva. It is believed that Lord Shiva appeared as an endless pillar of fire, whose beginning and end could not be found by Lord Brahma and Lord Vishnu. The lighting of lamps during this festival represents the remembrance of this divine manifestation and the presence of God’s eternal light.

The grand celebration of Karthigai Deepam takes place at the famous Arunachaleswarar Temple in Tiruvannamalai, where a huge sacred lamp is lit on the top of the Arunachala Hill. Devotees decorate their homes with oil lamps, worship Lord Shiva and Lord Murugan, and pray for peace, prosperity, and spiritual growth. The festival represents devotion, divine energy, inner awakening, and the removal of darkness from life.

Festival Month

Karthigai (November – December)

Significance / Importance

Karthigai Deepam holds great religious and spiritual significance in Hinduism. The festival celebrates Lord Shiva’s divine Jyoti form and honors Lord Murugan as a symbol of courage, wisdom, and devotion. Lighting lamps is considered a sacred act that removes darkness, ignorance, and negative energies while welcoming knowledge, purity, and divine blessings.

Devotees worship Lord Shiva and Lord Murugan to seek happiness, peace, success, protection, and spiritual progress. The festival teaches the importance of faith, devotion, righteousness, and the eternal victory of light over darkness.

Puja Vidhi

1. Wake up early, take a holy bath, and wear clean clothes.
2. Clean and decorate the home and पूजा area.
3. Worship Lord Shiva and Lord Murugan with devotion.
4. Light oil lamps (Deepam) and offer them to the deities.
5. Offer flowers, fruits, incense, and Prasad.
6. Chant Shiva Mantras and Murugan Mantras.
7. Light lamps around the house to celebrate the festival of divine light.
8. Pray for peace, prosperity, happiness, and spiritual growth.

Related Katha / Mantra / Aarti / Chalisa

* Karthigai Deepam Katha
* Shiva Mantra
* Maha Mrityunjaya Mantra
* Murugan Mantra
* Shiva Aarti
* Shiva Chalisa
* Skanda (Kartikeya) Stotra

faq.a1: Karthigai Deepam is celebrated on the full moon day of the Tamil month of Karthigai.
faq.a2: It is celebrated to honor Lord Shiva’s divine Jyoti form and worship Lord Murugan.
faq.q3: Where is Karthigai Deepam mainly celebrated?
faq.a3: Karthigai Deepam is mainly celebrated in Tamil Nadu and by Tamil communities worldwide.
faq.q4: Why are lamps lit on Karthigai Deepam?
faq.a4: Lamps are lit to symbolize the victory of light over darkness, knowledge over ignorance, and positivity over negativity.
faq.q5: Which deities are worshipped during Karthigai Deepam?
faq.a5: Lord Shiva and Lord Murugan are primarily worshipped during this festival.
faq.q6: What is the connection between Arunachaleswarar Temple and Karthigai Deepam?
faq.a6: The Arunachaleswarar Temple in Tiruvannamalai hosts one of the grandest Karthigai Deepam celebrations, where a massive lamp is lit on Arunachala Hill.
faq.q7: In which month is Karthigai Deepam celebrated?
faq.a7: It is usually celebrated in November or December.
faq.q8: What is the spiritual significance of Karthigai Deepam?
faq.a8: Karthigai Deepam symbolizes divine light, wisdom, devotion, spiritual awakening, and the removal of negativity from life.
